text: Kembali. Reversible stablecoin payments on HashKey Chain, live on mainnet.
Stablecoin payments are final. If the merchant never delivers, your money's gone. No chargeback.
Kembali is the recourse HSP doesn't have. Escrow, bound to an on-chain deliverable and a deadline.
This is the live dApp. Let's connect a wallet.
Flow one, the happy path: an atomic delivery-versus-payment.
The buyer mints some test stablecoin.
Then opens an escrow. A merchant, an amount, and an on-chain deliverable.
One click approves the token. The next signs an EIP-712 HSP mandate.
Funds are now held on-chain. The mandate's digest is the payment ID.
Now we switch to the merchant's wallet to deliver.
The merchant approves the deliverable, then hits fulfill.
Released. One transaction. Asset to the buyer, funds to the merchant. Atomic.
But what if they don't deliver? The money comes back.
New escrow, a short thirty-second window.
Watch. If the buyer tries to refund right now…
It reverts. Too early. The buyer can't rug the merchant mid-window.
The deadline is enforced on-chain. So we wait for the window to pass.
And now the refund goes through.
Refunded. The money came back. Recourse HSP alone doesn't give you.
Agent-safe commerce. Funds are pulled from the signer, never the submitter.
An agent, or a relayer, can buy on your behalf, and can't redirect a single cent.
One click: provisioned and protected. An agent literally cannot rug you.
And compliance is enforced on-chain. KYC and sanctions, checked before the escrow can even open.
Everyone built a gate in front of the payment. We built the door back out. Kembali, live on HashKey Chain.
